title: IPv4 Addressing, CIDR, VLSM, and Subnet Design · Incident Defense
summary: Create an address plan for departments, infrastructure, guests, point-to-point links, growth, and route summarization. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Convert IPv4 octets and masks between binary and dotted decimal and explain contiguous prefix bits.
Identify RFC1918, APIPA, loopback, unspecified, limited broadcast, multicast, and public addressing behavior.
Calculate network address, broadcast address, usable host range, prefix length, and address count for CIDR blocks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, vlans, dhcp pools, static infrastructure, and ipam ownership.

content.text: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Ipv4 Address Plan, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: An address spreadsheet is complete even when it omits VLAN and DHCP relationships. This fails the Net Ipv4 Address Plan contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: CompTIA Network Plus N10-009 Exam Objectives (N10-009 v4.0) — Controls every certification domain, objective, acronym, tool, protocol, and performance-based scope decision.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.

hints: Name the Net Ipv4 Address Plan precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An address spreadsheet is complete even when it omits VLAN and DHCP relationships.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Ipv4 Address Plan applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.
